vocabulary.example: Go straight and you'll find it.

vocabulary.meaning: A recognizable place used as a reference
vocabulary.example: The mosque is a famous landmark.

phrases.example: How do I get to the bus station?

phrases.bangla: বাম দিকে / ডান দিকে মোড় নাও

phrases.example: The station is about 5 minutes away.

phrases.bangla: তুমি মিস করবে না / সহজেই পাবে
phrases.usage: When a place is easy to find
phrases.example: It's a big building — you can't miss it!

sentences.en: Could you tell me how to get to the hospital?
sentences.bn: আপনি কি আমাকে হাসপাতালে যাওয়ার পথ বলবেন?

sentences.en: Go straight for two blocks, then turn right.
sentences.bn: দুটি ব্লক সরাসরি যান, তারপর ডানে মোড় নিন।

sentences.en: The bank is next to the post office.

quiz.explanation: An intersection is where two or more roads cross each other.

quiz.q: How do you ask for directions?
quiz.explanation: 'How do I get to...?' is the standard way to ask for directions.
This phrase means the place is very visible and easy to find.
'Turn left' means to turn to your left side.

story.title: Lost in the City
story.body:
Karim was new to the city. He needed to find the central hospital but he was lost.

He stopped an old man on the street. "Excuse me, sir. Could you tell me how to get to the central hospital?"

The old man smiled kindly. "Of course! Go straight down this road. After about 5 minutes, you'll see a big intersection."

"Then what?" asked Karim.

"Turn right at the intersection. The hospital is a large white building on your left. You can't miss it!"

"Is it nearby?" asked Karim.

"Yes, it's about 10 minutes on foot," said the man.

Karim thanked him and followed the directions. Sure enough, he found the hospital easily!

comprehension.q: Which direction does Karim turn at the intersection?

listening.title: Asking for Directions
listening.script:
Tourist: "Excuse me! Do you know where the train station is?"

Local: "Yes! It's not far. Go straight down this road."

Tourist: "How long will it take?"

Local: "About 10 minutes on foot. After the mosque — that's the big landmark — turn left."

Tourist: "Turn left after the mosque. Got it!"

Local: "Then go straight. The station is a large building. You can't miss it!"

Tourist: "Is there a pharmacy nearby? I need medicine."

Local: "Yes, there's one right next to the station entrance."

Tourist: "Perfect! Thank you so much!"

Local: "You're welcome! Have a safe journey!"

comprehension.q: What is the tourist looking for?
What landmark does the local mention?

speaking.prompt: Describe how to get from your home to your school or workplace. Use: go straight, turn left/right, you'll see, it's nearby.
speaking.example: From my home, go straight for 3 minutes. Then turn right at the intersection. You'll see a school on your left. My workplace is nearby.
tips: Use sequencing words: first, then, after that
Mention landmarks to make directions clearer
Speak slowly so the listener can follow

conversation.scenario: A tourist asks you for directions

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Excuse me! Can you help me find the nearest ATM?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Say yes and start giving directions.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Do I need to turn anywhere?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Tell them to turn at a specific place.

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Tell how far it is and say they can't miss it.
